questions.text: What is the primary purpose of over-collateralization in DeFi lending?

options.text: To protect the protocol from borrower default by ensuring the collateral value exceeds the loan value.
To reduce the amount of crypto in circulation.

questions.explanation: Over-collateralization ensures the protocol always has more value locked than borrowed, so even if a borrower defaults, the collateral sale covers the debt.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Over-collateralization ensures the protocol always has more value locked than borrowed, so even if a borrower defaults, the collateral sale covers the debt.

questions.text: On Aave, a health factor below which value triggers liquidation?
questions.explanation: Health factor < 1.0 means your collateral value (adjusted for liquidation threshold) is less than your debt, making your position eligible for liquidation.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

Health factor < 1.0 means your collateral value (adjusted for liquidation threshold) is less than your debt, making your position eligible for liquidation.

questions.text: What happens to the interest rate in a utilization-based model when utilization exceeds the optimal level (kink)?

options.text: It increases sharply to discourage borrowing and attract deposits.
It decreases to encourage more borrowing.

questions.explanation: The kink creates a steep rate increase above optimal utilization to prevent liquidity crises and incentivize repayments.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

The kink creates a steep rate increase above optimal utilization to prevent liquidity crises and incentivize repayments.

questions.text: Which of the following is a key advantage of deploying lending protocols on MST rather than Ethereum?

options.text: Extremely low gas fees make micro-loans and frequent compounding economically viable.
MST does not require collateral for loans.
MST uses a different interest rate model that guarantees profits.

questions.explanation: Sub-paisa fees remove the economic barrier to small loans and active management, opening DeFi lending to a global audience.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Sub-paisa fees remove the economic barrier to small loans and active management, opening DeFi lending to a global audience.

options.text: An additional percentage of collateral taken by the liquidator as a reward for repaying the debt.
A fee charged to the liquidator for performing a liquidation.
A penalty paid by the protocol to the borrower.

questions.explanation: The liquidation penalty (e.g., 5%) incentivizes liquidators to monitor the protocol and close unsafe positions.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

The liquidation penalty (e.g., 5%) incentivizes liquidators to monitor the protocol and close unsafe positions.

questions.text: "If you deposit ₹1,00,000 worth of ETH into Aave, you can safely borrow ₹80,000 worth of USDC because that's the maximum LTV."
questions.explanation: 1 mark: Correctly states FALSE. +1 mark: Justification explains that max LTV leaves no safety buffer, a small price drop triggers liquidation, and prudent LTV is much lower.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

1 mark: Correctly states FALSE. +1 mark: Justification explains that max LTV leaves no safety buffer, a small price drop triggers liquidation, and prudent LTV is much lower.

questions.text: "Deploying a lending protocol on MST allows users to borrow without providing any collateral, because the low gas fees reduce the risk for lenders."
questions.explanation: 1 mark: Correctly states FALSE. +1 mark: Justification explains that collateral is a risk requirement, not a gas issue; flash loans are the only exception but are not standard lending.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

1 mark: Correctly states FALSE. +1 mark: Justification explains that collateral is a risk requirement, not a gas issue; flash loans are the only exception but are not standard lending.
